name: González Lacasa Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de González Lacasa, Peñaloscintos, El Rasillo – ruta circular
description: Hike the difficult 11.6-mile (18.6 km) Embalse de González Lacasa circular route, gaining 1283 feet (391 metres) with reservoir and mountain
region_intro: Hiking around Embalse González-Lacasa, situated at an altitude of 1,000 meters in the Sierra de Cameros, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, "green mountains" such as Mojón Alto, and the clean waters of the reservoir itself. Trails often feature rebollo oak and gall oak groves, contributing to varied natural scenery. The area provides a blend of reservoir views, forested paths, and mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Embalse González-Lacasa The most popular hiking route is González Lacasa Reservoir Loop, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 15 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers expansive reservoir and mountain views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Olmo de El Rasillo – Ortigosa de Cameros loop from El Rasillo de Cameros, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail explores the area around El Rasillo and the town of Ortigosa de Cameros. Local hikers also love the González Lacasa Reservoir loop from Peñaloscintos, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with reservoir views, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Embalse González-Lacasa is defined by pine forests, reservoir views, and oak groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy access paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Embalse González-Lacasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse González-Lacasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: There are 14 hiking trails around Embalse González-Lacasa. These routes vary in difficulty, offering options for different experience levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What types of landscapes and natural features will I encounter on the hikes?
A: The trails around Embalse González-Lacasa traverse diverse landscapes, including extensive pine forests, rebollo and gall oak groves, and valuable kermes oak areas. You'll also experience stunning views of the clean, deep waters of the reservoir itself, surrounded by 'green mountains' like Mojón Alto and San Cristóbal.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy hiking routes. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo – Ortigosa Iron Bridge loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 5.9 km long and offers a gentle introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir loop from Peñaloscintos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 10.1 km. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989524169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.6 km trail offering expansive reservoir and mountain views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. These include the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo&lt;/strong&gt;, the charming town of &lt;strong&gt;Ortigosa de Cameros&lt;/strong&gt;, and the historic &lt;strong&gt;Puente Ra&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Ermita de San Mamés&lt;/strong&gt; in El Rasillo, a Romanesque hermitage, also offers a cultural stop.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for hiking, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn offers stunning fall foliage. These seasons are also known for micology, with permits available for mushroom gathering. Summer is also popular, especially for combining hikes with water activities.

Q: Are there opportunities for water activities or swimming in the reservoir?
A: Yes, the Embalse González-Lacasa is a popular spot for water activities. The Club Náutico El Rasillo offers aquatic sports such as pedal boating, canoeing, SUP, windsurfing, and light sailing. There are also designated areas for swimming and fishing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: The trails in Embalse González-Lacasa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful reservoir views, and the peaceful atmosphere of the pine and oak forests.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, there are 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989524169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 18.6 km trail with an elevation gain of 391 meters, offering extensive reservoir and mountain views, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might be limited, the region is accessible. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for connections to towns like El Rasillo de Cameros or Ortigosa de Cameros, which serve as starting points for many routes.

Q: Is parking available near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages surrounding the reservoir, such as El Rasillo de Cameros and Peñaloscintos, which are common starting points for many hiking routes. Look for designated parking areas near trailheads.
tips: Better known by local people as “El swamp del Rasillo” or “El Pantano de Ortigosa”, it is located at an altitude of 1,000 meters, surrounded by pine forests, in the Sierra de Cameros in La Rioja. There are good views, and the possibility of bathing, sunbathing, eating and having a drink.
Just a dozen houses, on a hillside and surrounded by nature. It has its charm.
A small town, but with good tourist services, in the area of Los Cameros and northwest of the González Lacasa reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de González Lacasa, Peñaloscintos, El Rasillo circular route is approximately 18.6 kilometers long and generally takes about 5 hours and 15 minutes to complete. This duration is for hiking and assumes a steady pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is officially categorized as 'difficult' due to its considerable length and elevation gain of around 390 meters. It requires very good physical condition and is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse de González Lacasa circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain features a mix of natural surfaces, including well-trodden paths, forest trails, and mountainous sections. You'll traverse green mountains and extensive forests, primarily pine, but also oak and beech in some areas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: Hikers are treated to picturesque views of the deep, cold waters of the González Lacasa Reservoir, nestled amidst vast pine forests. The route offers panoramic vistas of surrounding green mountains like Mojón Alto, San Cristóbal, and La Agenzana.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the González Lacasa Reservoir itself, the charming settlement of Peñaloscintos, and the notable Olmo de El Rasillo. The route also offers views of the surrounding mountains and the historical context of the reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, and winter hiking might require specific gear due to potential snow or ice at higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Embalse de González Lacasa circular route?
faqs.answer: The route passes near El Rasillo, which is a good starting point. Parking is generally available in or around the village of El Rasillo, providing convenient access to the trail.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in a more rural area of La Rioja. While specific public transport options directly to the trailhead might be limited, checking local bus services to El Rasillo village would be the best approach.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de González Lacasa circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to practice leave-no-trace principles by cleaning up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular route around the Embalse de González Lacasa.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes near El Rasillo, a village offering various amenities. Additionally, the Club Náutico El Rasillo, easily accessible from the area, provides opportunities for water sports, bathing areas, a restaurant, and a cafeteria.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating, considerable length, and varied terrain, you should wear appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended.
